implementation_notes: 230 instances (one per symbol) with class-level coordination. Each instance computes its own indicators, first timer aggregates cross-sectionally.
is_symbol_qualified returns True for all universe symbols from strategy/universe.csv
All indicators computed from accumulated bar buffers at runtime — no precomputed signals, no Polygon data, no embedded files
Daily bars from md.bar.daily_by_index(), accumulated in class-level buffers across multiday run
Persists across days in multiday job — no on_save/on_restore needed. Proven in v14 base strategy.
Start 2 years before trade_start to accumulate bar history. ~500 warmup trading days.
First date trades are allowed. Before this: accumulate bars, compute signals, but place NO orders.
252 KITE bars on day 1 (Jan 2018) + ~500 accumulated days = ~750 total closes per symbol by Jan 2020.
Same as original spec
~500 warmup (2018-2019) + ~1570 live (2020-2026). All multiday sequential.
Full run including warmup period
Read all ~252 KITE bars on first day. Extract opens/highs/lows/closes arrays by iterating until close==0.
Each subsequent day: read latest formed bar, append to buffer. Buffer grows by 1 bar per day.
Class-level dict keyed by symbol. Persists across days in multiday job.
bars.length is buffer capacity (86400) not actual count. Must read values and stop at first zero.

value: compare last stored close vs new bar close
implementation_notes: Prevent double-counting if on_start reads same bar as yesterday's append.
Simple arithmetic mean of last 50 closes
Efficient O(n): add close[i], subtract close[i-50]. Computed from accumulated buffer, not KITE's 252-bar window.
Need 50 bars for first SMA. Available by day ~50 of warmup.
10-day percent change of SMA50. Positive = rising, negative = falling.
SMA50 needs 50 bars + 10 more for slope.
Look back up to 252 slopes from accumulated buffer. Full lookback achieved after ~312 bars accumulated (~day 60 of warmup after seed).
NaN if fewer than 60 slope values in window.
window = slopes[-min(252, len(slopes)):] — includes today's slope. Matches pandas .rolling(252).mean() default. DO NOT shift.
std = sqrt(sum((x-mean)^2) / (n-1)). Matches pandas .std() default.
Each symbol z-scored against its own history, NOT cross-sectional.
Quantile boundaries with linear interpolation at 20/40/60/80 percentiles. Value-based assignment, not rank-based.
Same interpolation as numpy/pandas default. Verified against quant's first 5 trades.
Compare each z-score against 4 computed boundaries. Avoids rank-based bin edge errors.
5 quintile groups required
Skip day if fewer than 20 valid z-scores
If any two boundaries are identical → fewer than 5 groups → skip day. Equivalent to pd.qcut(duplicates='drop') failing.
quintile[t] == 5 AND prev_quintile[t-1] != 5. First day of each Q5 stint.
Cannot signal on first day with valid quintile — no prev_quintile available.
Skip signal if symbol already has an un-exited trade. Track via _open_position_symbols set. Reduces trades by ~10%.
Computed on signal day = last formed daily bar in KITE's on_start.
The day BEFORE entry. In KITE: yesterday's formed bar available via daily_by_index(start=-1).
If high == low, body_pct is undefined. Skip this trade entirely.
body_pct <= -0.53 → SKIP trade ($0). Fixed boundary from spec.
-0.53 < body_pct <= -0.17 → $500,000 (0.5x). Fixed boundary.
body_pct > +0.57 → $2,000,000 (2.0x). Fixed boundary.
Both numerator and denominator scale equally with split. body_pct is identical on adjusted and unadjusted data (same vendor).
Verified against quant's reconciliation. KITE vendor OHLC rounding may cause rare boundary flips.
Wilder's exponential smoothing (alpha = 1/14).
Standard True Range computed from accumulated buffer OHLC.
Simple average of first 14 True Range values. Seed for Wilder's smoothing.
Match to 4+ decimal places on reconciliation trade.
ATR value from the last formed daily bar (same as body_pct and stop computation).
Need 14 TRs (15 OHLC bars) for first ATR value.
3 ATRs below signal day's intraday low. Computed from accumulated buffer.
Place real algo_sell limit at stop_price the day AFTER entry. Fills through KITE's matching engine at exact stop_price.
Sell Limit ARCA at Script Price. Real order, real position, real P&L, real trade log.
Day after entry: place resting limit. NOT placed on entry day — matches spec 'check starts day 1 after entry'.

value: KITE fills naturally when market trades at/below stop_price
implementation_notes: Backtester simulates limit fill at stop_price. Fill price = exact stop_price, matching spec's theoretical assumption.
Must cancel resting stop limit before placing time exit market sell to avoid duplicate/conflicting orders.
When resting limit fills: event.intent='exit', remove position from _positions, remove symbol from _open_position_symbols.
Boolean flag prevents duplicate stop limit orders on subsequent on_start calls. Set True after first placement.
Signal fires in on_start (yesterday's bar = signal day). Entry at open+2s timer via market buy.
Market order fills near open. Not exact Polygon open price, but close.
Round down to whole shares. Share count differs from spec due to unadjusted price.
Buy Market ARCA. Real order through KITE matching engine.
Entry day = day 1, exit at close of day 20. days_held threshold = 21.
Matches quant's reconciliation exactly. 20 trading days after entry.
days_held incremented once per trading day via _initialized/_ranking_done reset check. Not per bar, not per callback.
Cancel resting stop limit before placing time exit market sell.
Market sell near close. Not exact daily close, but close.
Sell Market ARCA
Each day compare buffer's stored last close to KITE's version of that same bar. If ratio <0.6 or >1.7 → split detected.
On split detection: discard accumulated buffer, re-read all 252 KITE bars (now retroactively adjusted by KITE).
After re-seed: ~192 slopes available instead of 252. Returns to full lookback after ~60 trading days of re-accumulation.
status: ~5 symbols affected: AAPL TSLA GOOGL AMZN SHOP

implementation_notes: Log split events for post-hoc verification.
md.bar.daily_by_index returns no valid data for halted/non-trading symbols.
Cannot compute z-score without bars. Symbol excluded from _z_signals dict.
Symbol not in quintile sort that day. Matches spec: 'if a date is missing, that symbol isn't in the quintile sort'.
Existing positions continue counting hold days. Resting stop limit stays active on exchange regardless of bar availability.
No z-score → cannot enter Q5 → no entry signal for that symbol on that day.
Entry at md.L1.last (open+2s) vs spec's exact Polygon open price. Small difference (~0.1%) due to 2-second delay and bid/ask dynamics.
Exit at market (close-30s) vs spec's exact daily close. Small difference (~0.05%).
floor(size / unadjusted_price) vs floor(size / adjusted_price). For split stocks: AAPL shares = floor($1.5M/$320) = 4687 vs floor($1.5M/$80) = 18750.
KITE and Polygon may have slightly different OHLC values (rounding, different exchange feeds). Can cause body_pct to flip at quintile boundaries on rare occasions.
Due to remaining price/OHLC differences, some trades will differ. Core signal logic (z-score, quintiles, transitions) now matches spec methodology exactly.
2-year warmup (2018-2019) accumulates 500+ bars. Full 252-slope lookback by trade_start.
pd.qcut equivalent with linear interpolation boundaries. Verified against quant's first 5 trades.
Resting sell limit order at stop_price. Real order, fills at exact stop_price through KITE matching engine.
days_held=1 on entry, threshold=21. CRM entry 2020-01-10 exits 2020-02-10. Exact match.
Detect via price ratio, re-seed buffer from KITE's adjusted bars. ~5 symbols affected, temporary impact.
mean(TR[1]...TR[14]). MRK ATR14=1.0534 matches quant's 1.053433.
Split-neutral ratio. First 3 trades match quant. Will verify 10 trades in quick test.
0 bars → excluded from quintile sort. Positions keep tracking. Matches spec.
